Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 042 [Gilgameš Letter][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a]-⸢na TI⸣ [...] x [...]

(o 1) Say to ... [...] ... [...]:

o 22

⸢um-ma⸣ d⸢GIŠ⸣-[gim₂-maš ...]

(o 2) Thus says Gilgameš [...], creation of Anu [...], who, like a [...] string, [...] and the kings [...] thrones kiss [...]; the king who [...] all the lands from [...] sunset [...].

o 33

⸢bi⸣-nu-ut d⸢a-nu⸣ [...]

o 44

ša₂ ki-ma qe₂-⸢e⸣ [...]

o 55

u₃ LUGAL-MEŠ [... BARA₂]-⸢MEŠ u₂⸣-[na-aš₂-ša₂-qu ...]

o 66

LUGAL ša₂ ul-⸢tu₂⸣ [... e-reb] dUTUši KUR.KUR NIGIN-⸢ši⸣-[na ...]

o 77

⸢um⸣-ma ⸢a⸣-[na ...] dBAD ⸢na⸣-[aš-pan-ti-im-ma]

(o 7) Thus, at [...] of Ellil-of-Devastation:

o 88

[...] x lu₂EREN₂-MEŠ SIG-MEŠ 6 ME-TA.AM₃ aš₂-pu-⸢rarak-kam₂⸣-[ma ...]

(o 8) [...] ... I sent you 6 hundred prominent troops [and ...]

o 99

[...]-MEŠ GAL-MEŠ ša₂ na₄ZU₂ na₄ZA.GIN₃ KU₃.SI₂₂ liq-ti ⸢uḫ⸣-[ḫu-zu]

(o 9) I wrote to you [...] the great [...] of obsidian (and) lapis lazuli plated with liqtu-gold, to attach [...], and you said: "There are none".

o 1010

[...] ša₂ ra-ka-si ⸢aš₂-pu-ra⸣-kam₂-ma ia-aʾ-nu ⸢taq⸣-[bi]

o 1111

[... al-tap-rak]-ka u₄-⸢mu⸣ ṭup-⸢pu⸣ šu-a-te ta-⸢mu⸣-[ru]

(o 11) [...] I am writing to you (again). The day you see this tablet, [...] go [...] and take a team of horses with you!

o 1212

[... a]-⸢lik⸣-ma KI-⸢ka⸣ il₄-⸢lat ANŠE⸣.KUR.RA-MEŠe šu-⸢pur⸣-[ma]

o 1313

[... tak]-⸢ri-ṣi⸣ [ša₂] ⸢ki⸣-ma UR.MAḪ da-an ti-bu-šu₂-[un]

(o 13) [n] gnawing away [...] whose attack is as strong as a lion's;

o 1414

[...] ⸢ANŠE⸣.KUR.RA-MEŠ ⸢BABBAR-MEŠ⸣ ša₂ mi-kir-šu₂-nu ṣal-[mu]

(o 14) [n] white horses whose girth-straps are black;

o 1515

[...] ⸢ANŠE⸣.KUR.RA-MEŠ ⸢GE₆⸣-MEŠ ša₂ mi-kir-šu₂-nu ⸢pe-ṣu⸣-[u₂]

(o 15) [n] black horses whose girth-straps are white;

o 1616

[...] ⸢lim munus⸣ANŠE.KUR.RA-MEŠ ša₂ KUŠ-ši-⸢na⸣ <pa-an> ka-nak-ti KURi ⸢šak⸣-[nu]

(o 16) [n] thousand mares whose skin is formed (like) the surface of a mountainous incense-bearing kanaktu-tree;

o 1717

[...] lim AMAR-MEŠ TUR ša₂ ⸢da⸣-ka-ka la i-⸢kil-lu-u₂⸣

(o 17) [n] thousand young calves whose gamboling doesn't stop;

o 1818

[...] lim ṣi-⸢in-da-at⸣ pa-re-e bit-[ru]-mu-ti

(o 18) [n] thousand teams of piebald mules;

o 1919

[...] ⸢AMAR⸣-MEŠ [tak]-⸢di⸣-ri ša₂ ṣu-pu-ru bu-⸢un-nu-u₂ qar⸣-[nu] šal-mu

(o 19) [n] spirited calves whose hooves are beautified, whose horns are intact;

o 2020

[...] lim DUG I₃.NUN.NA 1.20 [lim dugku-ut]-⸢tu giš⸣GEŠTIN-MEŠ

(o 20) [... n] thousand pots of butter, 80 [thousand] jugs of wine;

o 2121

[...] ⸢SAG.DU⸣ giš⸢BANŠUR⸣-[MEŠe ...] ⸢ṣu⸣-lum gišta-⸢ti⸣-[di]

(o 21) [...] tabletops of the black wood of a tatīdu-tree;

o 2222

[...] ⸢te⸣-ram-ma al-⸢ka⸣

(o 22) [...] gather (all this) [...] and come.

o 2323

[... den]-⸢ki⸣-du₃ ib-ri-ia lu-⸢kin⸣  ; [... ina] ⸢muḫ-ḫi⸣ lu-⸢ban⸣-ni

(o 23) I want to fix [...] of Enkidu, my friend; on (it) I want to fashion [...].

o 2424

[... GAL]⸢e⸣ d⸢AMAR⸣.UTU

(o 24) [...] of the great [...], Marduk.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[... be₂?]-⸢e⸣-[ru ... ta?]-⸢aq?⸣-nu

(r 1) [...] selected, [...], good,

r 22

[... ia]-a-⸢la i-ip?⸣-pu-[šu₂]

(r 2) [...] will make stags.

r 33

[...] ⸢ši⸣-pir E₂.⸢KUR⸣ [...] ⸢ip-pu⸣-[šu₂]

(r 3) [...] he will do work for the temple [...].

r 44

[...] ⸢ma-ti⸣-e ⸢IGI-MIN⸣-[MEŠ]-⸢a⸣-[a]  ; [...] ⸢SUMa u₃⸣ ri-⸢qa⸣

(r 4) [...] my eyes [...] never [...], given or empty.

r 55

[... a]-⸢na a⸣-ḫa-meš ⸢qur-rib⸣

(r 5) [...] bring (them) together!

r 66

[... KU₃.BABBARim]-⸢ma KU₃.SI₂₂⸣ ana ⸢id₂⸣BURANUN!(UD.NUN)⸢ki ne₂-qel-pa-a⸣

(r 6) [...] float [... the silver] and gold down to/on the Euphrates!

r 77

[...] ⸢li-iḫ-mu⸣

(r 7) [...] may be stunned.

r 88

[... at-ma]-⸢ru⸣-ka

(r 8) I will see you [...].

r 99

[... la up-ta-sa-as]-⸢su⸣

(r 9) [... cannot] be annulled,

(8 lines missing)
r 1818

⸢u₂⸣-x [...]

(r 18) ... [...]

r 1919

ar-ḫa-[...]

(r 19) ... [...]

r 2020

⸢ši⸣-pir-ti [...]

(r 20) Letter [...].

r 2121

ŠU-MIN m[...]

(r 21) Hand of [...].
